evidence: Each frame has one load-bearing element — the probe stills in F1-2, the lilac dial in F3-4, the mint aha sentence anchoring the settled dial in F5-6.
The busiest frame (F2) shows three parallel probe stills of the same idea plus a score, well under four novel elements.
Labels 'along/across/against', 'oppose/ignore/agree', and 'a · b' all sit directly on their arrows, dial positions, and readout with no legend box.
On-screen text is limited to labels, the 'a · b' term, and the short annotation 'dot product = similarity meter' beside the dial and readout.
All text — dial labels, scores, and the aha sentence — is well above the ~2.2% height threshold and clearly readable.
The dial, arrows, readout, and sentence all sit inside the frame with margins on every side.
No text or graphic overlaps another; the aha sentence sits clear below the readout and dial.
Periwinkle vectors and 'a · b', lilac meter dial, amber needle/heat, and mint aha sentence all follow token semantics consistently.
Every element — arrows, scores, dial, needle, annotation — carries propositional content with no decoration.
The three probe stills replay with frozen scores, collapse into a lilac dial whose needle swings oppose/ignore/agree, and the mint aha sentence with periwinkle 'a · b' true up — matching the intent.
'a · b' sits directly above its score, labels touch their dial positions, and the aha sentence is adjacent to the readout.
The sequence builds in reasoning order: stills, then dial with swinging needle, then filled dial and mint aha sentence, reading as continuous morphs.
The composition has generous negative space and a clear top-to-bottom scan path from dial to readout to sentence.
